steps.title: Establish the relationship between quarter years and years
steps.content:
The original equation uses $t$ in years. The new equation uses $q$ in quarter years. Since there are 4 quarters in 1 year, we have:
$$t = \frac{q}{4}$$

steps.title: Substitute the time conversion into the original equation
steps.content:
Replace $t$ with $\frac{q}{4}$ in $M = 1800 \times 1.02^t$:
$$M = 1800 \times 1.02^{\frac{q}{4}}$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
